<20260408105941.3.de5411d00ec20fc9@mg2.s.subject: Rep. Gonzales Getting Expelled From Congress?
<20260408105941.3.de5411d00ec20fc9@mg2.s.text_content:
View this post on the web at https://www.currentrevolt.com/p/rep-gonzales-getting-expelled-from

As if having to suspend one’s campaign isn’t enough, one southwest Texas congressman may face additional penalties in Congress. Rep. Tony Gonzales dropped out of the race after news surfaced last year that a staffer set herself on fire following an affair with him.​​​​​​​​​​​ 
The staffer passed away. Now, many people are saying Gonzales should face greater consequences.
Via The Hill [ https://substack.com/redirect/1ce1e80a-97fb-4c6f-990e-a0afe0c8c098?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ]:
The Texas congressman ended his reelection bid [ https://substack.com/redirect/7830d978-ee09-48cb-9470-a79640a60d48?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] last month after calls from a wave of Republican colleagues and House Republican leaders, but he said he intends to serve out the remainder of his term.
A couple of House Republicans, though, are itching to see further discipline in light of the new allegations.
Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.), who has made her personal stories of facing sexual abuse core to her political identity, last month forced action on a resolution to publicly release all reports on file with the House Ethics Committee on investigations into members of Congress regarding allegations of sexual harassment or a sexual relationship with their staff. The House voted to refer that measure to the Ethics Committee, effectively killing it, with the committee warning it could create a chilling effect for victims and accusers.
“We haven’t forgotten the 357 members of Congress who voted to kill our resolution and keep congressional sexual harassment records buried, and you shouldn’t either when they ask for your vote,” Mace said Monday [ https://substack.com/redirect/525d44b3-c7c6-4246-a7cc-fe586c019008?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ].
Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) said she would support [ https://substack.com/redirect/fa29d8dd-2ef8-4c5a-a5ff-bbadd34df7a3?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] expelling Gonzales over the allegation.
“NO means NO. I’d vote to expel both him and Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick. Both need to go,” Luna posted Monday, in reference to the Democratic Florida congresswoman who an Ethics subcommittee recently found [ https://substack.com/redirect/b6bbc4ca-1683-4ac7-abce-7bd170eef868?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] had violated 25 counts of Ethics violations related to allegations she used millions in improperly paid federal disaster funds to finance her campaign.
The House Ethics Committee officially opened an investigation [ https://substack.com/redirect/9cb83f27-1ea0-44dc-9ab3-705ca2f04fbd?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] into Gonzales the day after his primary to examine allegations that he violated the House’s code of conduct, which prohibits members from having sexual relationships with staff.
That rule does not technically apply to campaign staff who are not government employees. But it is possible that the Ethics panel could expand its investigation to scrutinize that relationship under other ethics rules and standards.
As a result of the perversions first exposed by Current Revolt, a congressman now faces expulsion. If you’ve followed us awhile, you already knew Gonzales as an open borders activist censured by the Texas GOP, who accepted China-funded trips and called anyone to his Right a “Neo-Nazi.”
However, even worse personal details emerged later, culminating in a staffer self-immolating. Later, reporter Bayliss Wagner uncovered messages confirming Gonzales was involved in an affair with said staffer. During the resulting firestorm, Gonzales announced he wouldn’t seek reelection. Granted, it helped he already faced a runoff with popular activist Brandon Herrera.
If expelled, he’ll be the first Texan to be booted from the US House.

<1775653334628.1d0dc4e8-c554-463b-b50c-b.subject: Breaking: California Official Admits Audits "Are Not Getting Done" — "100% Fraud" in Homelessness Spending
<1775653334628.1d0dc4e8-c554-463b-b50c-b.text_content:
California Controller’s Press Secretary caught on camera: “Oh yeah, 100 percent” fraud exists… audits “are not getting done”… and there’s no statewide plan.

OKeefe-Wide-White-Shadowed-768x222 (https://d5XWn804.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/UF+113/d5XWn804/VVBc2-8wgVJxW29QBLj8f7Yx5W6-Tm8p5MDcZMN3Gk37C3lcq-W69sMD-6lZ3lMW36yxlC7Zs5nbW5pJtc97szkF2W5PPjGR5Gsg5-VnxT-31RNYHhMC7L04SPYYZW2FnC3S2pD9lNW6L4Fjz7CWzKMVK4Zds1zZWF8W59wbp25Kk8c3W2gtyM71D_LW5W8M1LpQ1pLCLSW7z4Q6k2R_dBtW31LmbT1zXxHLN5R9GdCrcnLkW1YdmhH7KkNl_N6xVkBYf3n30W5zQqh1526789W4sM_R82nw8V5W7XXf_l3xgk21W6cPYRZ1v43Jyf7YLM-004 )

CA Controller’s Office Official ADMITS Audits “Are Not Getting Done”

Thumbnail_BismarckObando (https://d5XWn804.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/UF+113/d5XWn804/VVBc2-8wgVJxW29QBLj8f7Yx5W6-Tm8p5MDcZMN3Gk38b3lcq-W7lCdLW6lZ3mJN5mykY22K_HFN1LWF2PvdrSfW9cCSqg2Z7GmSW9kXksr3XKq8JW4W49RR7n8s_cVHksrV8DY9frVSsDjy7cYxgfW30X46_48jmfvW60h9bG4ths4pW2SFK539hfWP_VL1whV5y0qvlW8bL5187vl2L7W7rx-Gs9kS6htW6wgJL22pxyGZW7LJF0j8k3szlW7zLsc62kmftkW2ttyxG5rLdhyW78xssF7dPqX7VkhvNh7Q9gHNW2C-Nfk2k2ND7W5fnWw_4QJ-GmW7DwGMx7msRt-W8Sndm08s4qPJVDzTf917jxD2f5Mx4zx04 )

Our undercover journalists have just exposed shocking admissions from inside California's own government about the state's homelessness crisis.

In part four of our investigation into California's homelessness spending, we confronted Bismarck Obando, Press Secretary for the California State Controller, on camera.

When pressed about why the Controller's office isn't conducting more aggressive audits of these massive programs, Obando admitted:

- Proactive audits are minimal
- Lawmakers are cutting auditing teams
- Some in power “don’t want to find out what the deal is”
- Audits “are not getting done” due to lack of resources

Obando also acknowledged there is “no statewide plan on homelessness”  even as cities and counties request another $1 billion this year just to keep the same programs running.

Meanwhile, our team visited nonprofits that have received tens of millions in taxpayer dollars:

-

Abundant Blessings; whose executive director, Alexander Soofer, was recently arrested by federal prosecutors for allegedly fraudulently obtaining over $23 million meant for homeless services. He now faces federal wire fraud charges and multiple California state felonies.

-

Urban Alchemy; which has secured over $60 million in government contracts, including more than $12 million through LAHSA since 2021. We visited their claimed “safe sleep villages” and mobile hygiene sites in Hollywood, Venice, and South Los Angeles — and found empty tents, closed-off areas, and severely under-utilized facilities despite the massive investment.

This is the reality of California's homelessness system: billions spent, nonprofits collecting tens of millions, one CEO allegedly living lavishly off public funds, vacant sites, rampant fraud admitted by a top state official, and zero real statewide plan to fix the crisis.

OMG will keep investigating these failures  including our ongoing work on the Skid Row petition fraud cases in cooperation with the Department of Justice.

<2oOJACO-QI6VBzxSLhCiqg@geopod-ismtpd-88.subject: Pillow Perfection: Explore the Best Pillows
<2oOJACO-QI6VBzxSLhCiqg@geopod-ismtpd-88.text_content:
[Sleep Foundation](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/)

[Sleep Foundation](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/)

 

[My Sleep Profile](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/my-sleep-profile)

[My Sleep Profile](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/my-sleep-profile)

[The Best Pillows of the Year](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/?link=hero-image)

Hi There,

Finding the right pillow shouldn’t be trial and error.

Our experts have tested, squeezed, and snoozed on pillows across the board to bring you the ones actually worth your money.

These are the pillows we recommend to friends, family, and you!

[See the Best Pillows of the Year](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/?link=button)

How Well Do You Know Your Pillow Needs?

[Take the Pillow Quiz](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/pillow-quiz/?link=button)

Your head spends nearly a third of your life on a pillow, so it’s more than just fluff.
 
It’s the foundation of proper alignment, pressure relief, and deep, uninterrupted sleep!

You wouldn’t wear glasses with the wrong prescription. So why sleep on a pillow that doesn’t support your body and sleep style?

Whether you're waking up with neck pain, dealing with shoulder tension, or just tired of flipping your pillow every night and you’re not sure where to start…don’t lose sleep over it!

There are better options out there and we’ll help you find it.

[Take our 2-minute Pillow Quiz](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/pillow-quiz) to get matched with options that fit your sleep style, support needs, and budget.

[Find My Perfect Pillow](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/pillow-quiz/?link=button)

[When Should You Replace Your Pillow?](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/how-often-should-you-replace-your-pillows/?link=image)

If your pillow is flat, lumpy, or you’re waking up with more aches than you went to bed with… it’s past its prime.

Pillows lose their shape and support over time.

In just a few years, your pillow can collect up to a third of its weight in dead skin, oil, sweat, and dust mites!

Your pillow should support your sleep, not sabotage it.

[Is Your Pillow Past Its Prime?](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/how-often-should-you-replace-your-pillows/?link=button)

Recommended Reads

[Best Pillows for Side Sleepers](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/best-pillow-for-side-sleepers)These pillows are built to support proper alignment and pressure relief all night long.

[Down vs. Feather Pillows](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/down-vs-feather-pillows)What’s the difference, and which is better for your sleep style?

[Best Pillows for Neck Pain](https://www.sleepfoundation.org/best-pillows/best-pillow-for-neck-pain)Expert-tested pillows designed to cradle your neck and ease tension where it matters most

<5A.26.32152.52C56D96@i-011930f30949b480.subject: Q1 Highlights: We are each other's resource
<5A.26.32152.52C56D96@i-011930f30949b480.text_content:
http://repower.org [http://repower.org]      Q1 2026      http://repower.org [http://repower.org]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
https://www.instagram.com/p/DVbg-33kjTQ/ [https://www.instagram.com/p/DVbg-33kjTQ/]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
Hello Benjamin,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
What if liberation was the baseline, not the bonus? That question isn't rhetorical for the communities and organizers re:power serves. It is the work. As our movements continue to shift power with a clear vision of what is possible, we are all being asked to interrogate our assumptions: about what is needed, who we place in leadership, and how sustained change actually happens. At re:power, the answer keeps coming back to the same truth: movements don't need heroes, they need leaderful communities.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
This quarter, re:power has stayed close to that work. We are deepening partnerships and designing custom training experiences built around what organizers and leaders are currently navigating on the ground. Take a look at the highlights from these past months and lookout for our upcoming trainings.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
re:cap                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/bisc%20repower4.png]      [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/cga%20wash5.JPG]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/ACL.png]                  [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/cga%20ohio.JPG]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/bisc%20repower2.png]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/nwhf.png]      Sharpening Electoral Strategy in Oregon and Washington                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
re:power launched an Electoral Justice Leadership Cohort with Northwest Health Foundation, bringing together leaders from Oregon and SW Washington for a kickoff convening in Portland.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
Over the next three months, cohort members will apply learnings directly to their 2026 electoral work. This is a space to pause, reflect, sharpen strategy, and learn about electoral organizing alongside peers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
Strengthening Collaborative Governance in Seattle and Columbus                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/cga%20wash4.JPG]            
In partnership with Local Progress and SAGE Leaders, re:power hosted a Collaborative Governance Academy [https://repower.org/classroom/cga/] training in Seattle for local elected officials. It was facilitated by sitting elected leaders, whose lived experience grounded the space in practical insight, honesty, and peer learning. Partners created effective relationship-building in real time. In Columbus, in partnership with Local Progress and Ohio Working Families Power, we delivered another CGA training for eight local elected officials who left with concrete next steps and a stronger sense of collective governing strategy.                                                                                                       
🗣️ “City council is a team sport!"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/bisc%20repower1.png]      Building Ballot Measure Power Across Three Regions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
The Ballot Initiative Strategy Center leverages ballot measures nationally to strengthen democracy, and this quarter, re:power was brought on to support their Regional Ballot Measure Leader Training program. We developed curriculum, prepared trainers, and supported delivery of both virtual and in-person sessions.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
Sharpening Narrative and Storytelling with Youth in WA                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/acl%20group%20pic.png]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
In February, re:power partnered with Asians for Collective Liberation to bring Story of Self, Narrative and Storytelling, and Mobile Messaging training to a cohort of high school students. Drawing from both our Grassroots Organizing and Movement Technology curriculum, the training prepared students to lobby their legislators in Olympia, WA.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/census%20repower.png]      Building Census Power with National Immigrant Rights Leaders                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
Thirty national and local organizations came together to discuss strategy and preparation for the 2030 Census, the American Community Survey, and state redistricting efforts. The Four Freedoms Fund, Census Equity Initiative, Fair Representation in Redistricting, and re: power convened immigrant rights leaders nationwide to discuss the surge in anti-immigrant attacks and how the current political climate will impact national and local participation.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
Resourcing Our People:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA003/1/75622/images/roproh%20stefanie%20t.png]                                                                                                                                                                                           
Remember our Power,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
Remember our Humanity                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
re:power hosted two virtual gatherings for alumni and friends, facilitated by healing justice practitioner Stefanie Tovar. The spaces offered somatic practices, reflection, and community care to ground and reconnect. In Part Two, we went deeper: sitting with our relationship to privilege and risk tolerance, naming what we each carry and what this moment is asking of us. We are each other's resource. That truth is what keeps movements going.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
re:fresh

<GTNdoFTyT0mugX60byiinQ@geopod-ismtpd-94.subject: ElevenMusic, Google Eloquent, and Heart Failure Detection by Voice
<GTNdoFTyT0mugX60byiinQ@geopod-ismtpd-94.text_content:
“_**It's Wednesday, April 8th**__: _This week, builders can generate full songs on their phone with ElevenMusic, capture speech with on-device AI using Google Eloquent, and study how Vox earned FDA Breakthrough status by detecting heart failure 21 days before hospitalization.“ — 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/09ed91dc-6e71-49ba-8e96-a97914adb848/aic-events-banner-2026-04-07.png?t=1775611998)
Follow image link: (https://www.aicollective.com/events)
Caption: 

Head over to our **[Events Portal](https://www.aicollective.com/events)**** **to get the latest on upcoming AI Collective events near you. Search by city, date, or event format, and join thousands of builders at events across 100+ chapters on every continent (except Antarctica, for now). 

“Find an event in your city using the link below.👇“ — 

Search Events (https://www.aicollective.com/events)

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ab4a9433-a745-4405-830a-1b26fafc99ee/Today_s_Top_Tools__1_.png?t=1774406516)
Caption: _In Today’s Top Tools, we spotlight some of the most innovative, creative AI apps that we recommend adding to your stack._

## 🎵 ElevenMusic: Turn a Text Prompt into a Full Song, Right on Your Phone

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/477d36a2-33da-429e-8a8d-4648ea002dc6/image.png?t=1775599043)
Caption: 

“ElevenLabs, the company behind some of the most widely used AI voice technology, launched ElevenMusic on April 1, a free iOS app that generates songs from natural language prompts. Describe a genre, set a mood, choose whether you want lyrics, and the app builds a full track in seconds. With a free tier covering seven songs a day and a $9.99/month Pro option, ElevenLabs is taking its first shot at mobile music generation, a space currently led by Suno and Udio.“ — 

[ElevenMusic](https://elevenlabs.io/music) runs on a single prompt input: describe genre, writing style, and mood, and the app handles the rest. The free tier allows seven songs per day. Songs can be remixed from other users' public tracks (remixes count toward the daily limit), and the app includes curated live stations and daily mixes organized by mood: Focus, Energy, Relax, Late Night, Cosmic, and Chill.

ElevenLabs raised a $500M Series C at an $11B valuation in February 2026. ElevenMusic is a clear move toward building a full audio platform, with voice cloning, narration, and music creation all in one product. For builders shipping audio-first or creator-facing apps, API access for music generation is a logical next step from ElevenLabs, and worth tracking.

Try ElevenMusic (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/elevenmusic-by-elevenlabs/id6755965224)

———————————————————————————

##  🎙️ Google AI Edge Eloquent: Dictation That Runs Entirely on Your Phone

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/2e31261f-3130-46ce-b284-40f7f168b426/image.png?t=1775599154)
Caption: 

“Google quietly pushed an iOS app called AI Edge Eloquent to the App Store on April 6, and its defining feature is what it doesn't do. It doesn't send your audio to the cloud. The app downloads Google's Gemma speech recognition model directly to your phone, transcribes everything locally, and requires no internet connection after the initial setup. It's free, with no usage caps, and goes directly at tools like Wispr Flow that depend on cloud uploads for every recording.“ — 

After a one-time download of the on-device [Gemma](https://ai.google.dev/gemma) ASR model, all transcription runs locally. No audio reaches Google's servers, no account is required, and it works fully offline. When you pause recording, the app automatically removes filler words ("um," "uh," mid-sentence corrections) and copies clean prose to your clipboard.

Eloquent is [Google AI Edge's](https://ai.google.dev/edge) consumer proof-of-concept for on-device ASR, but the underlying stack is open. [LiteRT-LM](https://ai.google.dev/edge/litert-lm), released this week, supports the same Gemma model family on Android, iOS, desktop, and Raspberry Pi. If you're building for healthcare, legal, or enterprise use cases where audio privacy matters, this is the model family worth integrating.

Try Google Eloquent (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/google-ai-edge-eloquent/id6756505519)

———————————————————————————

##  🫀 Vox: A Daily 5-Second Voice Recording That Screens for Heart Failure

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/dbeeded1-77d7-4a92-a72a-48512bd31ac9/Screenshot_2026-04-07_at_6.00.35_PM.png?t=1775599239)
Caption: 

“Heart failure causes over a million US hospitalizations each year, and the clinical problem is usually the same: patients deteriorate at home for days or weeks before anyone notices. Noah Labs built Vox to change that detection window. The system analyzes a 5-second daily voice recording to identify acoustic changes linked to cardiac congestion, sometimes 21 days before hospitalization becomes necessary. In April, the FDA granted Vox Breakthrough Device designation, fast-tracking US regulatory review.“ — 

Vox detects changes in vocal fold acoustics linked to pulmonary congestion and fluid overload, physiological shifts that appear in the voice before they become clinically obvious. The model was trained on more than 3 million voice samples and validated across five sites including the [Mayo Clinic](https://www.mayoclinic.org/) and [UCSF](https://www.ucsf.edu/). Patients record a 5-second clip daily through a phone; clinicians receive early-warning alerts when the model detects concerning patterns.

[FDA Breakthrough Device designation](https://www.fda.gov/medical-devices/how-study-and-market-your-device/breakthrough-devices-program) means the FDA has prioritized the review timeline, not that approval is granted. [Noah Labs](https://www.noah-labs.com/) expects to start its US clinical trial shortly. EU [MDR approval](https://www.noah-labs.com/nl-vox) is targeted for mid-2026. The product is currently in clinical validation, available to health systems and clinical research partners.

Try Vox (https://www.noah-labs.com/nl-vox)

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/aa18e9bd-9038-446f-be3a-10d443882d6f/Jobs_in_AI__2_.png?t=1774407937)
Caption: _In this section, we feature a few standout opportunities leading AI companies, non-profits, and other organizations. Each role is selected for impact and relevance to our community._

“🔬** Work at a Lab / AI Safety **

_Roles at frontier labs, safety organizations, and research institutions_ 

**[RESEARCH SCIENTIST](https://jobs.lever.co/aisafety/0e911ab2-89e0-4936-83e6-034f7e2f8977)** — [Center for AI Safety](https://safe.ai/) | San Francisco, CA (Competitive Salary)

**[SENIOR RESEARCH ENGINEER](https://jobs.lever.co/aisafety/e8167e84-8669-4644-961c-e5fd5b5f4318)** — [Center for AI Safety](https://safe.ai/) | San Francisco, CA (Competitive Salary)

**[FULL STACK CLOUD ENGINEER, AI SECURITY PLATFORM](https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/hiddenlayer/jobs/5100067007)** — [HiddenLayer](https://hiddenlayer.com/) | Remote, USA (Competitive Salary)

**[RESEARCH SCHOLAR](https://law-ai.org/career/research-scholar-2026/)** — [Institute for Law and AI](https://law-ai.org/) | Remote/Global ($100,000–$175,000 · closes Apr 17)

**[CHIEF OF STAFF](https://law-ai.org/career/cos/)** — [Institute for Law and AI](https://law-ai.org/) | Remote/Global ($130,000–$250,000 · closes Apr 24)“ — 

“🚀** Work in Industry**

_Roles at funded AI startups and private companies_ 

**[FOUNDING ENGINEER](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/agentmail/jobs/x66bmSk-founding-engineer)** — [AgentMail](https://agentmail.to/) | San Francisco, CA ($150,000–$250,000 + 0.5–2% equity)

**[FOUNDING ENGINEER, AI/ML](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/pax-historia/jobs/qK7Wj6I-founding-engineer-ai-ml)** — [Pax Historia](https://www.paxhistoria.io/) | San Francisco, CA ($150,000–$240,000 + 0.25–1% equity)

**[STAFF AI SYSTEMS ENGINEER](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/flock-safety/jobs/rd2Qy51-staff-ai-systems-engineer)** — [Flock Safety](https://www.flocksafety.com/) | Remote, USA ($200,000–$240,000)

**[STAFF AI ENGINEER](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/podium/jobs/qgKxAEM-staff-ai-engineer-usa)** — [Podium](https://www.podium.com/) | Lehi, UT / Remote ($190,000–$215,000)

**[FOUNDING AI ENGINEER](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/weave-3/jobs/SqFnIFE-founding-ai-engineer)** — [Weave](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/weave-3) | San Francisco, CA ($150,000–$200,000 + Equity)“ — 

“**🏛️ Work in AI Policy / Governance** 

_Roles in government, think tanks, NGOs, and policy organizations_ 

**[POLICY DIRECTOR](https://www.iaps.ai/policy-director)** — [Institute for AI Policy and Strategy](https://www.iaps.ai/) | Washington, DC / Remote ($141,000–$180,000)

**[SPECIAL PROJECTS ASSOCIATE](https://jobs.lever.co/epoch-ai/99d526d1-6b60-4e93-bb31-91000d7ef732)** — [Epoch AI](https://epochai.org/) | Remote, Global ($75,000–$100,000)

**[DIRECTOR OF POLICY](https://civai.org/jobs/director-of-policy)** — [CivAI](https://civai.org/) | Remote, Global ($130,000–$200,000)

**[DIRECTOR OF PROGRAMS](https://law-ai.org/career/director-of-programs-2026/)** — [Institute for Law and AI](https://law-ai.org/) | Remote/Global ($130,000–$240,000 · closes Apr 24)

**[DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS / COO](https://law-ai.org/career/director-of-operations-coo/)** — [Institute for Law and AI](https://law-ai.org/) | Remote/Global ($150,000–$280,000 · closes Apr 24)“ —

<640642309.7105179.1775664215247@sjmktma.subject: Explore news, tools, and events for startups
<640642309.7105179.1775664215247@sjmktma.text_content:
Discover the latest benefits, use cases, and best practices for cutting-edge startups. News for Startups April 2026 NVIDIA GTC 2026: The Biggest Moments From San Jose Explore highlights from the keynote, new releases, and can't-miss moments from NVIDIA GTC 2026. Get the Recap Tools, Resources, and New Releases
Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On‑Device with Gemma 4 Gemma 4 launches with multimodal, multilingual features and immediate integration across NVIDIA, Hugging Face, vLLM, Ollama, llama.cpp, and Unsloth. Learn More ❯
Achieving Single‑Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets Discover how the NVIDIA Grace Hopper Superchip in the Supermicro ARS‑111GL‑NHR server achieved record‑breaking inference speeds in the STAC‑ML Markets (Inference) benchmark, providing performance comparable to or better than specialized hardware systems. Learn More ❯
Maximize AI Infrastructure Throughput by Consolidating Underutilized GPU Workloads GPU partitioning is becoming standard in Kubernetes as NVIDIA MIG and time‑slicing enable more scalable, efficient voice AI workloads. Learn More ❯
Designing Protein Binders Using the Generative Model Proteina‑Complexa NVIDIA releases Proteina‑Complexa, an open‑source generative model for protein binder and enzyme design, validated on challenging molecular targets. Learn More ❯ Inception Startup Ecosystem Highlights NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang Convenes AI's Open Model Vanguard at GTC The CEOs of Mistral, Perplexity, Cursor, Thinking Machines Lab, and others joined Huang on stage to map what comes next in the open frontier era. Watch the Highlights ❯ Droplet Biosciences Accelerates Residual Cancer Detection With NVIDIA Parabricks Droplet Biosciences, a diagnostics company focused on lymph-based liquid biopsy tests, uses lymphatic fluid 24 hours after surgery to quickly detect residual cancer-derived molecules. Read the Story ❯ NVIDIA Launches Nemotron Coalition of Leading Global AI Labs to Advance Open Frontier Models Leading innovators Black Forest Labs, Cursor, LangChain, Mistral AI, Perplexity, Reflection AI, Sarvam, and Thinking Machines Lab are inaugural members, helping shape the next generation of AI systems. Read the Blog ❯ NVIDIA and Global Robotics Leaders Take Physical AI to the Real World Physical AI leaders across robot brain developers, industrial and surgical robot giants, and humanoid pioneers are building on NVIDIA technology to develop and deploy physical AI at scale. Read the Blog ❯ AI Is a Five-Layer Cake NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang describes the five-layer AI cake: energy, chips, infrastructure, models, and applications. Each layer depends on the others to generate intelligence at scale. Read the Blog Upcoming Live and On-Demand Events New NVIDIA Training - Virtual Instructor-Led Workshops with certifications: Inception members save 50% on instructor-led training - Full schedule (Americas and EMEA) - OpenUSD Development Exam - NVIDIA Training Labs now available What's New With NVIDIA Certification: Catch the latest in NVIDIA certifications, including new and updated exams in physical AI, AI infrastructure and operations, and data science. Attendees will receive a 50% exam discount code. - Register Now (available in multiple time zones) Upcoming Conferences - April 6–9: HumanX (use this link for priority registration) - April 22–24: Google Cloud Next - April 29–May 1: TiEcon - May 7–9: AI+ Expo Join NVIDIA Inception for Startups Inception is a free program that meets you wherever you are in your startup journey. It's your guide to NVIDIA’s AI ecosystem, offering developer tools and training, preferred pricing from NVIDIA, offers from partners, and a global network to help you discover your AI opportunity, build your solutions faster, and grow your business. <2127342933.7450790.1775664718376@sjmktm.subject: Submit a session for GitHub Universe 2026
<2127342933.7450790.1775664718376@sjmktm.text_content:
Got a bold idea? Now's the time to let us know. <[[https://github.com]]> Make your mark on the future of software GitHub Universe is coming back to San Francisco on October 28 and 29, 2026. Submit a session idea so you can shape the conversation, or nominate someone you admire to take the stage. Apply to speak The GitHub Universe call for sessions is officially open from April 8 to May 1. Submit your session idea now to lead a conversation during our two-day global event. Here's a breakdown of what our sessions look like this year: - Demo-style sessions (show something): Product demos and Ship & Tell (new format this year) are for speakers who want to show what’s possible and share learnings. - Thought leadership (dive deep): Breakout Sessions, Panels, and Fireside chats are built for longer narratives, perspectives, and big ideas, with room for Q&A. - Interactive learning (share skills): Workshops and Sandbox sessions focus on guided, participatory learning where attendees can practice new methods in real time. Need help polishing your submission? Our submission guide <[[ https://reg.githubuniverse.com/flow/github/universe26/cfs/page/cfs-submissionguide?utm_source=github&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=cfs_launch ]]> has you covered with a sample abstract, audience insights from 2025, and best practices, so your proposal stands out. And if there’s someone you’d love to see on the Universe stage, you can nominate a speaker, too. Have a manager, CEO, professor, partner, developer, or community leader who would inspire Universe attendees? Send them our way. Registration for the main event isn’t open yet, but if you want to be first to know when tickets go on sale, sign up to get notified <[[ https://githubuniverse.com/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=cfs_launch ]]>. LinkedIn Instagram X TikTok YouTube Twitch Copyright 2026 GitHub, Inc. <c3f85904-85de-6fe3-162d-2c2b31cd0a3f@em.subject: The Daring Mission to Rescue a Stranded US Airman

<20260408165749.3.25928213b9adaba8@mg2.s.subject: Gavin Newsom’s $30 Billion Fraud Magnet
<20260408165749.3.25928213b9adaba8@mg2.s.text_content:
View this post on the web at https://christopherrufo.com/p/gavin-newsoms-30-billion-fraud-magnet

California Governor Gavin Newsom is embroiled [ https://substack.com/redirect/922c8dfd-4ba6-4fe2-937e-17f691ce3958?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] in a national fraud scandal. Thus far, much of the coverage has focused on alleged schemes related to unemployment [ https://substack.com/redirect/2ee9e8cc-2457-4e27-80d2-ac92595366c5?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] insurance, hospice care [ https://substack.com/redirect/ef1d7824-6568-44f0-b041-077cdd5bcef2?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ], and food stamps. In this exclusive investigation, we shine a light on one of California’s largest [ https://substack.com/redirect/96f6cfff-cae6-40cb-a6a3-840c107dd2f0?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] initiatives: the In-Home Supportive Services Program, or IHSS, which pays [ https://substack.com/redirect/fe65de2b-bad7-42fd-9fa1-54e332063405?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] family members and other individuals to provide home-based care for the elderly and disabled—at a cost [ https://substack.com/redirect/af1ba998-41a3-4660-96a9-36bba3bf12b6?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] of nearly $30 billion per year.
On the surface, IHSS presents itself as an instrument of compassion, directing billions to caregivers who help [ https://substack.com/redirect/28ea970f-20dd-446b-9286-5897a8a6e9d5?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] with cooking, personal care, laundry, and other daily needs inside recipients’ homes. But a growing number of experts and critics [ https://substack.com/redirect/4b9f15c4-182a-4077-aeef-755e8e8e4093?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] argue that the program is rife with fraud, losing roughly an estimated $6 billion to $12 billion yearly to scammers. Meantime, the state’s powerful home-care unions collect more than $149 million in membership dues, funneling money into the political network supporting Newsom and California Democrats.
This is the story of a government that has allowed compassion to become a mask for fraud, creating a self-reinforcing system that keeps the Democratic establishment in power.
In 1973, California created [ https://substack.com/redirect/8150d8db-e51f-4486-af85-8005897277ef?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] what became the IHSS program to provide in-home care to the elderly and disabled. The IHSS caseload exploded [ https://substack.com/redirect/f7257ff2-c458-4346-8fe4-8b48dbd18c1b?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] in the 1980s, prompting the state to impose hour caps on care providers. California offers [ https://substack.com/redirect/b1a437ce-7ada-4188-a823-de41a4d1e29f?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] the program through Medi-Cal, its version of Medicaid, and pays [ https://substack.com/redirect/abca054b-dee1-49a4-ae5c-03c6bca7adec?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] providers with a combination of federal, state, and county funds.
IHSS has long been considered a magnet for fraud. In 2009, then-Governor Arnold Schwarzenegger estimated [ https://substack.com/redirect/082a1bc1-fe14-4e11-ae04-612459fd5423?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] that up to 25 percent of IHSS claims were fraudulent. A Sacramento grand jury report that year found [ https://substack.com/redirect/6c31040f-1199-49e0-88fe-9e35b1d3f3d6?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] that providers had “no meaningful oversight, no assessment of skills to meet client needs, no monitoring of the validity of service hours, and no background checks.”
After growth of the program’s rolls exploded [ https://substack.com/redirect/a1292ca9-fa95-4fcc-8cf1-1a6233d2d077?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] in the early 2000s, Schwarzenegger signed [ https://substack.com/redirect/c1382b28-2e63-42af-b0a1-048e1b9ef2a0?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] legislation aimed at curbing abuse, requiring IHSS providers to undergo [ https://substack.com/redirect/6b1c486d-0d8c-4894-9bed-614fe5da291d?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] criminal background checks and introducing random claim reviews [ https://substack.com/redirect/8f464128-ad75-4c1a-985c-7dda55f32625?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ].
But soon after the law’s passage, a state workgroup, teaming with representatives from “labor organizations,” introduced [ https://substack.com/redirect/f1a87f89-4655-49a9-9aad-017303443415?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] a key loophole. Citing concerns about disruptions for “vulnerable members of the IHSS community,” the task force barred regulators from conducting fully randomized, unannounced home visits, leaving the program more exposed to scammers.
Since then, the program has expanded [ https://substack.com/redirect/3bcbbdbc-a755-41a9-8cd2-82bdea742714?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] dramatically again. “IHSS provider” has become [ https://substack.com/redirect/7fb5beba-0a0b-4d67-ad93-8b9e2dac2289?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] the largest low-wage occupation statewide, with more than 800,000 taxpayer-funded caregivers [ https://substack.com/redirect/0afbd4f8-ae92-462d-94b5-f1281760e797?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] offering [ https://substack.com/redirect/e1261f7c-d3ac-4113-b8bc-b6df22893c15?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] everything from grocery shopping to personal care.
Applying for IHSS care is straightforward. First, a prospective beneficiary files [ https://substack.com/redirect/a9592743-8bea-48fe-a321-3819ca36a58a?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] a California Department of Social Services application. Second, a county social worker visits [ https://substack.com/redirect/b7b5ae0b-24e9-4ba5-bc55-758f8770ee0a?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] his home to determine his need for care. Third, the county or public authority reviews [ https://substack.com/redirect/723fede4-0f51-42ac-942e-e7c49ebffe34?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] the application and, if it approves, designates the applicant as a beneficiary, who can then use taxpayer funds to hire the caregiver of his choice—in more than 70 percent [ https://substack.com/redirect/0afbd4f8-ae92-462d-94b5-f1281760e797?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] of cases, a direct family member.
The system operates largely on trust. Providers self-report their timecards and check-in records. In roughly 60 percent of cases, providers and beneficiaries live together [ https://substack.com/redirect/0afbd4f8-ae92-462d-94b5-f1281760e797?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ], delivering care in private homes—typically without the threat of random, unannounced visits.
County-level fraud controls appear to be lacking. State regulations prevent [ https://substack.com/redirect/f1a87f89-4655-49a9-9aad-017303443415?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] IHSS staff from making unannounced visits unless a whistleblower files a “specific” complaint or regulators identify clear red flags. Even when complaints are lodged, investigations apparently can be slow, and prosecutions slower still. According to the state’s Department of Social Services, for one 12-month period between 2023 and 2024, counties received [ https://substack.com/redirect/19a789e2-ec1a-4de5-981a-cf21a0f6b218?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] nearly 7,000 fraud complaints; 28 counties recorded 964 fraud investigations, resulting in just 39 cases prosecuted.
County-level regulators face additional problems. A 2021 Riverside County audit [ https://substack.com/redirect/abe8831a-47d6-4817-8c4e-7fbc4a7c945a?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] claimed that the local IHSS program did “not adhere to review and approval procedures for program integrity referrals.” In addition, more than 60 percent of county staff at the Department of Public Social Services, which, according to the auditor, handles program oversight, were IHSS providers themselves—meaning they had an interest in maintaining the status quo.
California, in other words, is sending billions [ https://substack.com/redirect/af1ba998-41a3-4660-96a9-36bba3bf12b6?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] of dollars per year to a program that is easy to exploit, difficult to administer, and almost impossible to supervise.
In 2024, the federal government began a series of enforcement actions. As part of a nationwide crackdown on health-care-related crimes, the United States Department of Justice announced prosecutions [ https://substack.com/redirect/c7c122ee-84e1-411f-b1fa-1740dc174159?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] for alleged IHSS fraud in California.
In one case, the DOJ alleged that San Dimas resident Giacomo Lorenzo Garbarino billed the state for more than $170,000 in fraudulent IHSS and Medi-Cal services over a five-year period. The patient in his care was reportedly hospitalized or living in a facility at the time and thus ineligible for IHSS reimbursement. In another case, Joseph Depiazza allegedly billed the state for more than $50,000 in fraudulent IHSS services. According to prosecutors, he kept submitting claims after the patient entered the hospital—and even after she had died.
Last year, federal officials announced [ https://substack.com/redirect/7cb7d3cd-7690-4f30-853a-1bb429273cd5?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] another round of IHSS fraud prosecutions. In one case, prosecutors alleged that Maryam Erambakhsh falsely claimed payments for caring for her parents while they were outside the United States. In another, Cindy Lynn Fromm allegedly billed the program for more than a year of services while the recipient was incarcerated.
These cases may be the tip of the iceberg. Haywood Talcove, CEO of LexisNexis Risk Solutions for Government and a nationally recognized fraud expert, estimates that annual IHSS fraud could amount to 20 percent to 40 percent of total program spending. Applied to projected IHSS outlays for fiscal year 2025–26, that suggests roughly $6 billion to $12 billion in losses. Multiple senior officials at the Department of Health and Human Services have described a similar pattern, estimating that about 25 percent of the Medi-Cal budget is lost to fraud. The share is likely higher for IHSS, given the program’s structure and its susceptibility to abuse.
Earlier this year, Mehmet Oz, head of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, set off a firestorm of controversy with the claim [ https://substack.com/redirect/c9a823ec-e938-4a37-a3d6-68881450b5be?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] that criminal networks, including the “Russian Armenian mafia,” were running massive hospice and home care scams in Los Angeles. He suggested [ https://substack.com/redirect/9692cca1-32e5-447b-bc34-3a3859348c5b?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] that Armenians in Los Angeles were disproportionately represented among the leaders of hospice fraud rings.
One red flag for social-services fraud, Talcove suggested, is high community usage rates, and Armenian speakers in Los Angeles do appear to be significantly overrepresented among the county’s IHSS providers and recipients. Armenian speakers represent 14.6 percent of IHSS recipients and 6.5 percent of IHSS caregivers, per state data. People who speak Armenian fluently and English less than “very well,” however, make up [ https://substack.com/redirect/561caf15-d5c5-45da-8d4a-89be5f68476b?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] just 0.8 percent of L.A. County’s population, according to Census data. Assuming that these Armenian speakers are identical to self-reported Armenian speakers in IHSS data, Armenian speakers who speak English less than “very well” are 18 times more likely to be IHSS recipients and eight times likelier to be IHSS caregivers than their share of the county population would suggest.
These high utilization rates do not, on their own, prove fraud. But analysts such as Talcove argue that “organized criminal groups” are “taking advantage of the program at scale.” Using a conservative 25 percent fraud estimate—within Talcove’s range and consistent with HHS officials’ broader Medi-Cal assessments—scammers may have siphoned at least $35 billion from IHSS during Gavin Newsom’s administration. “There’s virtually no incentive for them to stop,” Talcove said. “California has limited to no controls in place.”
Perhaps the biggest beneficiaries of California’s IHSS program are labor unions, which have enrolled [ https://substack.com/redirect/10c29060-9559-41e7-9879-e4d9480a54e6?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] more than 600,000 IHSS providers. Two leading unions for the state’s in-home care workforce, SEIU Local 2015 [ https://substack.com/redirect/a325f074-276d-435f-8d74-74aec45a8427?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] and United Domestic Workers (UDW [ https://substack.com/redirect/d92c72d6-dcf1-4a8f-ab12-c47812d78506?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ]), collectively raked [ https://substack.com/redirect/09336c6a-caf2-442f-8e0c-7b99445aeb7f?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] in [ https://substack.com/redirect/274f05eb-f05d-4321-9350-ea25f8b4c13a?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] more than $149 million in dues in 2024. Both have a direct stake in the program’s expansion, since every new IHSS worker is a potential [ https://substack.com/redirect/f974be4b-7361-4225-a096-acb6dc1968be?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] dues-paying member [ https://substack.com/redirect/228726ac-1aa3-477a-bc67-95189f47e87d?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ].
These unions are some of the most powerful institutions in California politics and collectively send millions of dollars to the state’s Democratic establishment. SEIU Local 2015 contributed [ https://substack.com/redirect/8d256051-772f-4ff2-af62-9c8b1b53d85b?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] $2 million to Newsom’s anti-recall campaign; spent [ https://substack.com/redirect/1385bdfb-18d2-4d61-9e67-9a97520a0df3?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] another $2 million in favor of Proposition 50, a measure to redraw [ https://substack.com/redirect/f66f9365-65c9-47ce-9326-f7916165e248?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] the state’s congressional districts in favor of Democrats; and donated over $90,000 to Newsom across his two [ https://substack.com/redirect/c3ef242a-8cfb-4382-9461-1f008e133330?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] campaign [ https://substack.com/redirect/ba01f306-705a-420a-999f-5eda22d152c1?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] years. UDW donated [ https://substack.com/redirect/7795b84b-5a20-432d-b397-5280c1951905?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] $20,000 to Newsom’s 2022 reelection campaign. Both unions have contributed [ https://substack.com/redirect/7a5c01fc-af04-4143-9199-db133c9443e3?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] heavily [ https://substack.com/redirect/7795b84b-5a20-432d-b397-5280c1951905?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] to Democrats.
SEIU Local 2015 and United Domestic Workers of America have also allegedly used coercive tactics to expand their membership. Nathan Vu, a caregiver in San Diego, says that UDW pressured him to join during his IHSS orientation in February 2024. Though he says he declined, the union allegedly began deducting dues from his paycheck anyway.
“I vividly remember not wanting to enroll in the union,” Vu said. “I specifically said no.” Vu says he told UDW to stop deducting dues and demanded that they provide a copy of his membership agreement and proof of authorization. UDW, he claims, provided neither, and kept deducting dues for a full year before terminating his membership.
UDW did not respond to our request for comment.
SEIU 2015, which claims [ https://substack.com/redirect/f9c92955-1a67-4faf-869d-733ed968b080?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] to represent “thousands of long-term care workers,” has allegedly one-upped UDW. According to one former member, the group locked IHSS workers in a room and “coerced” at least one to become a member.
In a sworn statement [ https://substack.com/redirect/a3a71da4-45f7-4f8c-bca7-824cfa7370c7?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ], filed as part of a lawsuit against the union, Chaquan May alleges that, in September 2023, the union “coerced” her into signing up for a membership at an IHSS orientation. May claimed that union employees locked the doors and told her and the other new IHSS providers, “we’re waiting for everyone to sign . . . no one is leaving until everyone signs.”
May claimed to have signed under duress. On September 3, 2024, she alleged, the union began deducting dues. She claimed to have sent a certified-mail opt-out letter the following day, which the union allegedly ignored. SEIU Local 2015, which did not respond to our request for comment, allegedly continued deducting dues from her paycheck until at least April 2025.
Unfortunately for May and other allegedly mistreated IHSS workers, these disputes between public employees and their unions are handled by the California Public Employment Relations Board (PERB). Each of the board’s five members [ https://substack.com/redirect/79137b27-d4d4-42e3-bee8-dac5e0a8dcf3?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] are appointed by the governor, and three of the current members have former union ties.
“The Public Employment Relations Board is run by former union officials,” said Shella Alcabes, a lawyer representing May and other plaintiffs. “You think I’m going to get a fair hearing there? Absolutely not.”
The system itself seems designed to prevent accountability. Governor Newsom is responsible for all the main components of the IHSS operation: he oversees the program, the enforcement, and, effectively, the Public Employment Relations Board. He has no incentive to crack down on a program that is enriching his most powerful allies, and, according to coauthor Schrupp’s reporting [ https://substack.com/redirect/2c5ff1b0-4ffc-401e-8a44-3138114e35d0?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ], is responsible for more than 40 percent of the net jobs created during his administration. And so, the system expands.
Despite making some initial noise about reforming the program last year, for this upcoming budget season, Governor Newsom has sought [ https://substack.com/redirect/881b465f-b4a5-457b-87b6-c70c88b8853b?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] to increase IHSS funding by an additional $1.1 billion, to a total of $33.4 billion, boasting [ https://substack.com/redirect/b4614cf6-1a79-45c2-9a6f-62abb85c0cf0?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ] that his administration has invested billions of state taxpayer dollars into the program. (When we reached out to Newsom’s office for this story, a California Department of Social Services spokesman said that “[f]raud in the IHSS program is not widespread.”)
Given these political realities, the only plausible check on IHSS may come from Washington. The Trump administration could condition federal funding on rigorous audits and structural reforms. HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. could require California to mandate re-registration of all caregivers and recipients using third-party identity verification and expand random, unannounced visits.
Absent such intervention, the corruption in California is unlikely to abate. It will take a countervailing authority—willing to confront not only fraudsters but also the political system that sustains them—to force meaningful change.

Unsubscribe https://substack..yQhVVWYsJS5EfQ08UGrfTD72eqKL5VhiUvwF6KPs83k?

<cc91e559-8ecc-4679-b68d-f464c106e842@at.subject: ASUS Liquid Cooling Solutions for Optimal AI Performance
<cc91e559-8ecc-4679-b68d-f464c106e842@at.text_content:
If you do not want to receive this email again, please click unsubscribe from all emails at the bottom. Thank you!

									 

https://www.connect.asus.com/e/922413/us-ai-ai-infrastructure-/3dd4pg/593899252/h/CcYpygabut_b6wDsWVrqqmCmcPhjg5eC4oVOGwASO3o

									Optimal thermal management is the key to efficiently scaling your AI infrastructure and unlocking performance headroom. Click here

https://www.connect.asus.com/e/922413/e-ai-liquid-cooling-solutions-/3dd4pk/593899252/h/CcYpygabut_b6wDsWVrqqmCmcPhjg5eC4oVOGwASO3o

 to learn more about why air-based cooling isn’t sufficient to support the desired performance density of a modern AI factory. 

In short, AI innovation and performance scaling is outpacing thermal management in many cases. Increased GPU density is generating unprecedented heat, rising energy consumption leads to wasteful Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) and operational costs, and subpar cooling solutions are slowing deployment and scaling.

									 

									​ASUS offers a comprehensive portfolio of liquid cooling solutions designed for enterprise AI and HPC:  

Direct-to-Chip Cooling (D2C): Cold plates are placed directly on CPUs and GPUs to boost performance, extend hardware lifespan, and reduce energy consumption; ideal for sustained, high-density workloads. 

Direct-to-Chip Cooling with In-Row CDU: Combines chip-level precision cooling with centralized row control to maximize thermal efficiency and simplify large-scale AI deployment. 

Liquid-to-Air Cooling: Absorbs system heat via liquid and dissipates through air, providing a flexible upgrade path compatible with existing infrastructure.  

Liquid cooling enables the scaling of high-density workloads. By supporting greater computational density, it allows enterprises and research initiatives to accelerate complex AI tasks without being constrained by heat or inefficiency. Some of the key benefits include:

Optimized AI performance supports higher-speed and more complex workloads without compromise.

Improved PUE reduces energy waste and increases operational efficiency.

Lower operational costs minimize energy consumption and infrastructure strain.

https://www.connect.asus.com/e/922413/i-pod-nvidia-vera-rubin-nvl72-/3dd4pn/593899252/h/CcYpygabut_b6wDsWVrqqmCmcPhjg5eC4oVOGwASO3o

									ASUS has a variety of flagship systems that showcase the full potential of liquid cooling and the advantages it offers, including: 

ASUS AI POD with NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72

https://www.connect.asus.com/e/922413/i-pod-nvidia-vera-rubin-nvl72-/3dd4pr/593899252/h/CcYpygabut_b6wDsWVrqqmCmcPhjg5eC4oVOGwASO3o

 – A 100% liquid-cooled, rack-scale powerhouse engineered for massive AI workloads, featuring NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 GPUs. With up to 227 kW TDP, it is purpose-built to handle trillion-parameter AI models in large-scale AI factories. 

XA NR1I-E12L / E12LR

https://www.connect.asus.com/e/922413/d-nvidia-hgx-rubin-gpu-server-/3dd4pv/593899252/h/CcYpygabut_b6wDsWVrqqmCmcPhjg5eC4oVOGwASO3o

 – Hybrid and full liquid-cooled options, balancing D2C GPU cooling with air-cooled CPUs for seamless infrastructure transitions. 

HGX Rubin server series, XA NB3I-E12

https://www.connect.asus.com/e/922413/ervers-gpu-servers-XA-NB3I-E12/3dd4py/593899252/h/CcYpygabut_b6wDsWVrqqmCmcPhjg5eC4oVOGwASO3o

, ESC8000A-E13X

<0100019d6e2042ce-0af5f1d6-7952-4f1f-b03.subject: Credit-based pricing coming to Daydream
<0100019d6e2042ce-0af5f1d6-7952-4f1f-b03.text_content:
https://daydream.live/?utm_source=loops&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=pricing

 
Hi everyone,
 
I wanted to give you an early preview of some billing changes that are coming to Daydream Scope and the Daydream API.
 
First, the important thing: Scope will still be free to run locally, and that's not changing. If you're running everything on your
own hardware, the rest of this email doesn’t apply to you. =)
 
For users of cloud inference in Scope and the Daydream API (including the hosted version of StreamDiffusionTD), we're introducing
a credit-based pricing system later this month. Current users will receive a limited credit stipend each month, which will provide
around an hour of usage (exact duration varies by GPU type). If you expect to exceed this allotment and want to prevent your
streams from being interrupted, we'll offer several monthly subscription tiers (with top-ups available) tailored to different
workflow types and GPU needs.
 
There's nothing you need to do right now. We're still working through the details on pricing and will share specifics before the
new system goes live.
 
One thing that would be really helpful: if you're running a commercial installation or working on a client-facing project, please
reply to this email. We'd love to hear more about how you're using Daydream so we can make sure the new tiers make sense for your
workflow.
 
Thanks for being part of the community,
 
Chris
You are receiving this email because you opted-in to receive updates from Daydream
Daydream, 223 Bedford Ave PMB 530, Brooklyn, NY 11211

<20260408180257.3.731b733a630b6807@mg-d0.subject: 23 More Academic Scandals
<20260408180257.3.731b733a630b6807@mg-d0.text_content:
View this post on the web at https://www.chrisbrunet.com/p/23-more-academic-scandals-c28

#1: Samuel Vigne Update
Last month I wrote a story about Brian Lucey getting 13 papers retracted:
My article hit #1 on Hacker News: 
https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47119530 [ https://substack.com/redirect/384a83fa-f17a-4c4b-ad46-19c2cf67b5e0?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ]
Today’s update involves professor Samuel Vigne, Lucey’s protégé, and former PhD student, who has now had yet another paper retracted:
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1059056023001764 [ https://substack.com/redirect/93f56d18-0f28-4e25-8ab0-82438a350251?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ]
The stated reason for this new retraction is fraudulent citations:
The journal, with guidance from an impartial field expert acting in the role of an independent Publishing Ethics Advisor, found that 22 citations were added to this paper at the article revision stage without justification to the handling editor. Additional 8 citations were added to this paper at author proof stage without the approval of the Editor. The new citations were inserted generically without being thoughtfully integrated into the paper’s narrative, thus lacking direct relevance to the specific points being made. The new citations mainly benefit the authors Muhammad A. Naeem and Sitara Karim.
#2: MIT Fraud Update 
Another popular article I wrote was about a PhD student at MIT named Aidan Toner-Rodgers, who was kicked out of school for fabulously fabricating data. 
Another paper by Aidan Toner-Rogers (MIT) was just retracted a few days ago, this time in the top journal in the world for Development Economics:
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0304387826000714 [ https://substack.com/redirect/a05f608a-1016-4654-8cf5-e39cd5d011fa?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ]
The stated reason for this new retraction is because his co-author requested it:
This article has been retracted at the request of the corresponding author (FLF).
The corresponding author has indicated that key results in the paper cannot be independently verified and that the reliability of the underlying analyses cannot be confirmed. In light of these concerns, the Editors have concluded that the findings cannot be relied upon and have therefore retracted the article. This retraction is issued to maintain the integrity of the scientific record.
#3: Florian Ederer Update
Long-time readers will remember that I gave a seminar at Stanford University in 2023 about a hacked economics forum:
Here we are 3 years later, and the resulting paper from the hacked data is still not published, stuck in R&R limbo. Last week, I asked the authors for an update:
I am guessing it is held up due to legal issues. 
#4: Faculty Leaks
This week I came across Faculty Leaks , an “anonymous dispatch from inside higher education”. It’s a brand-new account—just launched this week—and they’ve already published their first 2 pieces. I’m always glad to support new writers, and this one looks promising. If you’re interested in academia, this Substack is worth checking out and subscribing to.
#5: Plagiarized Poison Ivy ...

Unsubscribe https://substack..U_fDupw8X6nirDC8naNBMZscSnPgQHmGHnqd7IP51a4?

[https://ssl.gstatic.com/docs/doclist/images/icon_10_generic_list.png] sam barber restraining order.pdf<https://drive.google.com/file/d/1hhzVVCLj1LXYKhm9-enQ5R-CzFzNoXwJ/view?usp=drive_web>

Attached is a restraining order that your client has been avoiding being served on, I have sent a paper copy of the same restraining order to your office via FedEx. Consistent with your duty of honesty to the court, you should have mentioned that there was already an ongoing case or controversy between the same parties, and that it was subject to collateral estoppel.

However I believe that there is reason to believe that your client was less than honest with you, given that he has previously expressed his opinion that he was free to violate a restraining order, because it had no effect while he was avoiding service, and he might have hid that fact.

Many of the problems that he complains about in his petition to the court, are harms that derived from him attempting to usurp power as a power of attorney, for example temporarily having me removed from the lease of our apartment, despite the fact he was under a protective order.

You should now make efforts to ensure that he preserves all evidence that is related to the parties to the controversy, and third parties that are related in some way or another with the parties to the controversy, regardless of whether those records are in your possession, possession of your agents, or other third parties. This would include GPS and positioning data, phone calls, emails, letters, and any other record such as contemporaneous notes, meeting minutes, or contracts, that may exist.

It is my intention to send the court a motion to show cause why you / your have failed to appear in the restraining order case, and a motion to show cause why your client has refused to turn over documents and follow the directives of the court, and I suggest that you have a "Back behind the woodshed" moment with your client regarding honesty and candor with the courts.

<20260408194757.3.8142d59b7d5deeda@mg2.s.subject: If Memory Safety is The Goal Rust is Not The Solution
<20260408194757.3.8142d59b7d5deeda@mg2.s.text_content:
View this post on the web at https://lunduke.substack.com/p/if-memory-safety-is-the-goal-rust

Every significant Rust program (GNU Coreutils re-write, System76's Cosmic Desktop, etc.) is filled with "unsafe" code.  If being Memory Safe was truly important, Rust is a terrible solution.
More from The Lunduke Journal:
https://lunduke.com/ [ https://substack.com/redirect/9d09755f-d7e6-4505-a318-befa4104d3cd?j=eyJ1IjoiaGs0dHgifQ.jnvJ1XKV5SYKjWxFtl5BNbWTOajovMTEeR95QTjQN60 ]

Unsubscribe https://substack..qzeorpQyYywYVuYvDxj_xrzYPl4K1wmQ9x0nEre-h-Q?

3. **OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google are now sharing intelligence to stop Chinese labs from stealing their models.** Three rivals, one non-profit, [one common threat](https://bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-06/openai-anthropic-google-unite-to-combat-model-copying-in-china) 🤝
